definitions.Clarity: How clear and understandable the paper is.
definitions.Novelty: The originality and innovativeness of the research.
definitions.Baseline and Comparison Limitations: Lack of sufficient baselines and comparative analysis with existing methods.
definitions.Experimental Rigor: The thoroughness and reliability of experimental validation.
definitions.Methodological Strength: The robustness and integration of the methods used.
definitions.Theoretical Soundness: The degree to which the research is backed by solid theoretical justification.
definitions.Practical Relevance: The applicability and impact of the research in real-world scenarios.
definitions.Scope and Generalization: The breadth of the research's applicability and its ability to generalize to other contexts.
definitions.Unverified Claims: Overstatements or assertions not backed by evidence.
definitions.Complexity and Scalability: The manageability of the research's complexity and its scalability over larger datasets or environments.
definitions.Documentation and Presentation: The quality and clarity of presentation, including all necessary details and explanations.
definitions.Related Work Gaps: Lack of engagement or analysis with existing literature.
definitions.Efficiency Concerns: Issues related to the computational or operational efficiency of proposed methods.
definitions.Assumption Transparency: Clarity and justification of underlying assumptions made in the research.
definitions.Reproducibility Issues: Challenges related to the ability to reproduce the results as indicated.
